lesson.menu_desc: Reading sustainability metrics and the EUDR timeline out loud -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 5 -- Numbers, Dates & Data: Reading Metrics and the EUDR Timeline
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Carlos Eduardo | Aula 5 -- Numbers, Dates & Data

listenings.text: Good morning. Let me present our Impact numbers. The Impact certification has eighty-two criteria. Eleven of them are critical. In two thousand twenty-three, we audited one hundred fifty farms. Today, about thirty percent of them have the full certificate. Each farm sends three documents per year. The most important date is the EUDR deadline: December thirtieth, two thousand twenty-four. On that day, every farm must be deforestation-free. That is our data, and that is our timeline. Thank you.

Hi Carlos, this is Emma with a quick note about the timeline. The EUDR is a European regulation. The deadline is December thirtieth, two thousand twenty-four. Before that date, we need geolocation data for every farm. In January, we start the supply chain mapping. On March fifteenth, we send the first report to the headquarters. At nine in the morning, Switzerland time, we have the alignment call. Please remember: in for the month, on for the day, at for the time. Thank you, and good luck with the numbers.

extra_audio.text: First, Carlos opens the data. The Impact program has eighty-two criteria. Eleven of them are critical. In two thousand twenty-three, the team audited one hundred fifty farms. Thirty percent have the certificate. The EUDR deadline is December thirtieth, two thousand twenty-four.
